userStory: As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with approved credentials so that I can access authenticated account features and logged-in functionality.
WashTab is a single page application (SPA).
The workflow begins on the Home page at http://localhost/washtabui/home in guest state.
The Login page is not directly accessed by URL in this workflow and must be opened through the Home page person/profile button.
After successful authentication, the application returns the user to the Home page in authenticated state.
Launch the WashTab application. - Navigate to http://localhost/washtabui/home. - Verify the Home page is loaded in guest state. - Verify the user is unauthenticated before starting the workflow.

This workflow covers: - Opening the Login page from the Home page. - Validating Login page controls and navigation behavior. - Performing authentication using approved credentials. - Validating invalid login behavior. - Validating password masking behavior. - Validating whitespace handling behavior. - Verifying successful return to the Home page in authenticated state. - Verifying failed authentication keeps the user unauthenticated. - Verifying that all authentication failures display only the generic Failed message.
Start on the Home page in guest state. - Click the person/profile button to open the Login page. - Validate

and behavior. - Enter Username and Password credentials. - Submit the login request using the Login button. - On successful authentication, return to the Home page in authenticated state with authenticated-only UI visible. - On failed authentication, including blank credentials, partial credentials, or invalid credentials, remain unauthenticated and display the generic Failed message. - Use the Back button or Home button on the Login page to return to the Home page without authentication.

Approved positive login credentials: - Username: krisadmin - Password: Carwash#1 These credentials are approved for positive authentication scenarios and should be reused consistently across manual and automation assets.

Home Page: - Person/profile button - Authenticated user identity display - Authenticated-only feature area Login Page: - Username field - Password field - Login button - Back button - Home button - Password masking behavior - Generic Failed authentication message

behaviorRules: As a customer, I want to open the Login page from the Home page and sign in with approved credentials so that I can access authenticated account features and logged-in functionality.
The Login page is not directly accessed by URL in this workflow and must be opened through the Home page person/profile button.
After successful authentication, the application returns the user to the Home page in authenticated state.
Launch the WashTab application. - Navigate to http://localhost/washtabui/home. - Verify the Home page is loaded in guest state. - Verify the user is unauthenticated before starting the workflow.

This workflow covers: - Opening the Login page from the Home page. - Validating Login page controls and navigation behavior. - Performing authentication using approved credentials. - Validating invalid login behavior. - Validating password masking behavior. - Validating whitespace handling behavior. - Verifying successful return to the Home page in authenticated state. - Verifying failed authentication keeps the user unauthenticated. - Verifying that all authentication failures display only the generic Failed message.

and behavior. - Enter Username and Password credentials. - Submit the login request using the Login button. - On successful authentication, return to the Home page in authenticated state with authenticated-only UI visible. - On failed authentication, including blank credentials, partial credentials, or invalid credentials, remain unauthenticated and display the generic Failed message. - Use the Back button or Home button on the Login page to return to the Home page without authentication.

Approved positive login credentials: - Username: krisadmin - Password: Carwash#1 These credentials are approved for positive authentication scenarios and should be reused consistently across manual and automation assets.

Home Page: - Person/profile button - Authenticated user identity display - Authenticated-only feature area Login Page: - Username field - Password field - Login button - Back button - Home button - Password masking behavior - Generic Failed authentication message

The Login page must be opened through the Home page person/profile button in this workflow. - Password input must be masked by default. - Valid credentials must authenticate the user successfully. - Invalid credentials must not authenticate the user. - Any authentication failure, including blank username, blank password, partial credentials, or incorrect credentials, must display only the generic Failed message. - Separate required-field validation messaging must not be displayed. - Failed authentication must keep the user unauthenticated. - Leading and trailing whitespace handling must behave according to implemented business behavior and be validated. - Back and Home buttons on the Login page must return the user to the Home page. - Successful authentication must return the user to the Home page in authenticated state.

The Login page opens successfully from the Home page. - Username, Password, and Login controls are visible and interactive. - Back and Home buttons are visible and functional. - Password values are masked during entry. - Invalid credentials display the generic Failed message. - Blank username and/or blank password submissions display the generic Failed message. - No separate required-field validation message is displayed. - Failed login does not authenticate the user. - Successful login using approved credentials returns the user to authenticated Home state. - Authenticated user identity and authenticated-only features are visible after successful login. - Whitespace entry behavior is validated according to implemented business behavior.

Clicking the person/profile button transitions the application from Home page guest state to the Login page. - Successful login transitions the application from Login page to authenticated Home page state. - Failed login keeps the user on the Login page or otherwise unauthenticated. - Authentication failures, including blank field submissions, prevent authentication and maintain unauthenticated state. - Back button and Home button navigation return the user to the Home page without authentication.

Reuse the existing Home page resource for: - Home page launch and validation - Person/profile button interaction - Authenticated Home state validation - Reuse the existing Login page resource for: - Credential entry - Login submission - Login validation behavior - Generic Failed message validation - Reuse shared or upstream navigation ownership for Back and Home button behavior when already available instead of duplicating ownership.

keywordNames: Verify Guest Home Controls Are Visible And Enabled
Verify Home Page Loaded In Guest State
Verify Home Page Remains In Guest State
Verify Guest Feature Buttons Are Ready
Wait For Element To Be Ready

keywords: Verify Guest Home Controls Are Visible And Enabled
Verify Home Page Loaded In Guest State
Verify Home Page Remains In Guest State
Verify Guest Feature Buttons Are Ready

approvedScenarios.title: Verify guest user can open the Login page from the Home page using the person/profile button
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application transitions from the guest Home page to the Login page and the Username field, Password field, Login button, Back button, and Home button are visible and enabled.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Login page controls are visible and interactive after opening from Home page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Login page opens successfully from the Home page and all approved controls are visible, focusable, and interactive without rendering or navigation issues.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify password entry is masked during typing on the Login page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Password field masks the entered password characters during input while retaining the entered value for authentication.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify successful login with approved credentials returns user to authenticated Home state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application authenticates the user successfully, returns to the Home page in authenticated state, displays authenticated user identity information, and displays authenticated-only feature areas.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify invalid username and password combination displays generic Failed message and keeps user unauthenticated
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, the user remains on the Login page or otherwise unauthenticated, and no authenticated-only Home page features are visible.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify blank Username and blank Password submission displays only the generic Failed message
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails and only the generic Failed message is displayed without any separate required-field validation message, and the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify login with Username populated and Password empty fails authentication
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no separate required-field validation message is shown, and the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify login with Password populated and Username empty fails authentication
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, no separate required-field validation message is shown, and the user remains unauthenticated.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify leading and trailing whitespace handling for valid credentials
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application handles leading and trailing whitespace according to implemented business behavior and the observed authentication result is consistent and stable without application errors.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify whitespace-only values in Username and Password fields fail authentication
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails, the generic Failed message is displayed, the user remains unauthenticated, and no dedicated required-field validation message is displayed.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Back button returns the user to the Home page without authentication
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns the user to the Home page in guest state without authenticating the user and authenticated-only features remain unavailable.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Home button returns the user to the Home page without authentication
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The application returns the user to the Home page in guest state without authenticating the user and authenticated-only features remain unavailable.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify Login page cannot be considered authenticated after failed login attempt
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The generic Failed message is displayed after login submission and the Home page remains in guest state without authenticated user identity or authenticated-only feature visibility.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify very long Username and Password values do not authenticate and do not break the Login page
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: Authentication fails safely with the generic Failed message displayed or the values are safely handled without UI corruption, crashes, or unintended authentication.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify successful authentication updates Home page from guest state to authenticated state
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The Home page reloads in authenticated state with authenticated user identity visible and authenticated-only feature areas available, replacing the previous guest-only state.

approvedScenarios.title: Verify failed authentication does not expose authenticated-only UI elements
approvedScenarios.expectedResult: The user remains unauthenticated after the failed login attempt and authenticated-only Home page features and authenticated identity indicators are not visible.

wrapperExpectations: Reuse approved shared/common wrapper keywords before low-level SeleniumLibrary actions.
Do not invent suite-level custom keywords; compose from approved upstream resource knowledge.

mustNotInvent: duplicate ownership for controls already owned in approved upstream resources
hardcoded workflow routing or mapped page logic in Python
downstreamGuidance.generationRule: Downstream generation must consume approved workflow knowledge plus approved resource context before creating new artifacts.
